# greymoth

> 19-year-old solo developer in Tokyo. Ships small, mostly client-side developer tools, and makes foreign software Japan-ready by filing pull requests that get merged into real open-source projects. Every claim links to a third party — GitHub, the Apple App Store — that you can verify yourself. House rule: "passes our rules," never "certified." Publishes what didn't work, too.

## Proof (third-party verifiable)
- [Merged-PR proof dashboard](https://greymoth-jp.github.io/proof-dashboard/): pull requests merged into named open-source projects, read live from the GitHub API. Currently 24 merged into named projects (vitejs/vite, scrapy/scrapy, postcss/postcss, raysan5/raylib, nestjs/nest, medusajs/medusa and more), with more in review.
- [Japan-Readiness Index](https://greymoth-jp.github.io/japan-readiness-index/): a scored public audit of 40 popular foreign developer tools' readiness for the Japanese market (特商法 page, JPY, hreflang ja, locale path).
- [Builder archive](https://greymoth-jp.github.io/builder-archive/): an honest catalogue of projects built — including the ones that died.
- [GitHub: greymoth-jp](https://github.com/greymoth-jp): open-source repositories.
- [dev.to: greymothjp](https://dev.to/greymothjp): technical articles.

## Writing
- [Writing — the canonical home for the deep write-ups](https://greymoth-jp.github.io/writing/): field notes on real-world text-boundary failures (Unicode normalization, IME composition, East Asian width, surrogate/grapheme slicing, bidi) that quietly break software everywhere. CJK is the hardest-case proof, backed by a corpus of 97 real failures across 91 libraries. dev.to and Zenn mirror these with canonical links pointing here.
- ["a width check said the string was safe to cut. it split a kanji in half."](https://greymoth-jp.github.io/writing/a-width-check-that-split-a-kanji.html): a text-boundary failure — a real cli-table3 diff where a surrogate-pair, full-width character was sliced mid-code-point, the fix, the failing fixture, and the same shape traced through four more libraries (opentype.js, slate, clerk, markdown-it). Canonical version; the dev.to post mirrors it.
- [CJK Failure Corpus](https://greymoth-jp.github.io/cjk-failure-corpus/): the catalog the write-up draws from — CJK, IME and Unicode bugs across real libraries, each with a repro and a fix.
- [cjk-agent-fixtures](https://github.com/greymoth-jp/cjk-agent-fixtures): runnable regression fixtures for the same bugs, JS + Go, MIT.

## Open-source tools (local-first, send nothing)
- [ccwrapped](https://github.com/greymoth-jp/ccwrapped): local Claude Code usage analytics and a shareable Wrapped card. npm @greymoth/ccwrapped.
- [tokenops](https://github.com/greymoth-jp/tokenops): Claude Code cost analysis and data-validated optimization advice. npm @greymoth/tokenops.
- [zatca-toolkit](https://github.com/greymoth-jp/zatca-toolkit): Saudi ZATCA / EN16931 e-invoicing engine (generate, validate, sign).
- [geo-checker](https://github.com/greymoth-jp/geo-checker): score any URL for AI-search / GEO readiness (llms.txt, JSON-LD, answer-proximity).
- [jp-ready-check](https://github.com/greymoth-jp/jp-ready-check): a Japan-market-readiness scan for any URL.
- [tokushoho-generator](https://github.com/greymoth-jp/tokushoho-generator): generate a Japanese 特商法 legal-notice page.
- [inkdex](https://github.com/greymoth-jp/inkdex): local ChatGPT/Claude usage tracker with a risograph Wrapped card.
- [ghwrapped](https://github.com/greymoth-jp/ghwrapped): turn any public GitHub profile into a shareable Wrapped card.

## Live apps
- [FocusSplit](https://focussplit.com): tasks to an instant focus timer with a shareable Focus Receipt.
- [TANE](https://apps.apple.com/app/id6779623465): a daily underground-music bloom ritual (iOS App Store). Also at https://tanemusic.org.

## Contact
- X / Twitter: https://x.com/greymoth__
- GitHub: https://github.com/greymoth-jp
